TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: izua ti Muakai kati Muatjuku, a mare sikatsekeɫ.
S.PHON: izua ti muakai kati muacuku, a marə sikaʦəkəʎ.
S.FORM: izua ti Muakai kati Muatjuku, a mare sikacekelj.
S.PHON: izua ti muakai kati muaʦuku, a marə sikaʦəkəʎ.
S.TRANSL: There were two cousins, Muakai and Muatjuku.

S.FORM: putsekeɫ ti Makuɫuɫu tjai Muakai; puaɫak tjai Sapulungan.
S.PHON: puʦəkəʎ ti makuʎuʎu cai muakai; puaʎak cai sapuɭuŋan.
S.FORM: pucekelj ti Makuljulju tjai Muakai; pualjak tjai Sapulungan.
S.PHON: puʦəkəʎ ti makuʎuʎu ʦai muakai; puaʎak ʦai sapuɭuŋan.
S.TRANSL: Makuɫuɫu married Muakai, and their child was Sapulungan.

S.FORM: putsekeɫ ti Sapulungan tjai Muatjuku; puaɫak tjai Muakai.
S.PHON: puʦəkəʎ ti sapuɭuŋan cai muacuku; puaʎak cai muakai.
S.FORM: pucekelj ti Sapulungan tjai Muatjuku; pualjak tjai Muakai.
S.PHON: puʦəkəʎ ti sapuɭuŋan ʦai muaʦuku; puaʎak ʦai muakai.
S.TRANSL: (Another) Sapulungan married Muatjuku, and their child was Muakai.

S.FORM: ɫakua a kutji sakamaya, neka nu kinatsavatsavan; ɫakua matsaqu a qivu.
S.PHON: ʎakua a kuci sakamaja, nəka nu kinaʦavaʦavan; ʎakua maʦaqu a qivu.
S.FORM: ljakua a kutji sakamaya, neka nu kinacavacavan; ljakua macaqu a qivu.
S.PHON: ʎakua a kuʦi sakamaja, nəka nu kinaʦavaʦavan; ʎakua maʦaqu a qivu.
S.TRANSL: But she was only a vagina, she had no body; but she could speak.
“Well, Muatjuku has had a girl.

S.FORM: ari tja ɫiɫavaw; uɫa kisudju ti Sapulungan."
S.PHON: ari ca ʎiʎavaw; uʎa kisuɉu ti sapuɭuŋan."
S.FORM: ari tja ljiljavaw; ulja kisudju ti Sapulungan."
S.PHON: ari ʦa ʎiʎavaw; uʎa kisuɉu ti sapuɭuŋan."
S.TRANSL: Let’s go and visit them, so that Sapulungan can court her.”
They went to pay a visit.
“Where is your child?” they said when they arrived.
She was hidden.
“There’s none.
Our child didn’t live.”
The visitors went back.
Sapulungan grew up.

S.FORM: vaik a qemaɫup tua uri sikisudju.
S.PHON: vaik a qəmaʎup tua uri sikisuɉu.
S.FORM: vaik a qemaljup tua uri sikisudju.
S.PHON: vaik a qəmaʎup tua uri sikisuɉu.
S.TRANSL: He went to hunt something as a courting present.
(His parents sent a message:) “We are coming to arrange a marriage.”

S.FORM: saka, "mangtjez; uri tja kudain sa tja aɫak a namaitutsu?
S.PHON: saka, "maŋcəz; uri ca kudain sa ca aʎak a namaituʦuʔ
S.FORM: saka, "mangtjez; uri tja kudain sa tja aljak a namaitucu?
S.PHON: saka, "maŋʦəz; uri ʦa kudain sa ʦa aʎak a namaituʦu?
S.TRANSL: So (her parents said:) “They are coming. What are we going to do about our child like this?
We’re ashamed of her.
Let’s hide her.”
They got a trunk and put her in that.

S.FORM: satjezen a pasa tsemtsemel itua ɫiv, sa vayvayin a itung anga.
S.PHON: sacəzən a pasa ʦəmʦəməɭ itua ʎiv, sa vajvajin a ituŋ aŋa.
S.FORM: satjezen a pasa cemcemel itua ljiv, sa vayvayin a itung anga.
S.PHON: saʦəzən a pasa ʦəmʦəməɭ itua ʎiv, sa vajvajin a ituŋ aŋa.
S.TRANSL: They sent it off to a cave in the woods, and hung clothes round it.
Sapulungan found the place.

S.FORM: "aku namaitutsu a imaza, a liaw a itung anga, qau neka nu tsautsau?"
S.PHON: "aku namaituʦu a imaza, a ɭiaw a ituŋ aŋa, qau nəka nu ʦauʦauʔ"
S.FORM: "aku namaitucu a imaza, a liaw a itung anga, qau neka nu caucau?"
S.PHON: "aku namaituʦu a imaza, a ɭiaw a ituŋ aŋa, qau nəka nu ʦauʦau?"
S.TRANSL: “Why is it like this, with lots of clothes and nobody here?”

S.FORM: patsunan nimadju a sema taladj tua ɫiv.
S.PHON: paʦunan nimaɉu a səma taɭaɉ tua ʎiv.
S.FORM: pacunan nimadju a sema taladj tua ljiv.
S.PHON: paʦunan nimaɉu a səma taɭaɉ tua ʎiv.
S.TRANSL: He looked inside the cave;
and there was the chest.
Sapulungan opened the lid.

S.FORM: manu izua i taladj a tsinavu.
S.PHON: manu izua i taɭaɉ a ʦinavu.
S.FORM: manu izua i taladj a cinavu.
S.PHON: manu izua i taɭaɉ a ʦinavu.
S.TRANSL: Inside there was something wrapped up.
“Don’t unwrap me. I’m ashamed.”
“Who are you?”
“I’m Muatjuku’s child.”
“Why are you here?”
“I was hidden by mother.

S.PHON: ʔmasiaq tua qaɭiqaɭi,ʔ aja tia kina."
S.TRANSL: Mother and the others said: ‘We’re ashamed of what others will say.’”
Sapulungan took her and put her in his net-bag (rucksack).

S.FORM: "anema, qalian, sa su kinavits a sadjelung?"
S.PHON: "anəma, qaɭian, sa su kinaviʦ a saɉəɭuŋʔ"
S.FORM: "anema, qalian, sa su kinavic a sadjelung?"
S.PHON: "anəma, qaɭian, sa su kinaviʦ a saɉəɭuŋ?"
S.TRANSL: “Friend, what’s that heavy thing on your back?”
“ɫakaraw flowers (for head decorations).”

S.FORM: vaik timadju a isangas qemalats, sa vaik a sema tjumaq.
S.PHON: vaik timaɉu a isaŋas qəmaɭaʦ, sa vaik a səma cumaq.
S.FORM: vaik timadju a isangas qemalac, sa vaik a sema tjumaq.
S.PHON: vaik timaɉu a isaŋas qəmaɭaʦ, sa vaik a səma ʦumaq.
S.TRANSL: He went ahead with a hunter’s shout of success, and went home.

S.FORM: semukavits, sa alapen a pasa surur, saka taqed.
S.PHON: səmukaviʦ, sa aɭapən a pasa surur, saka taqəd.
S.FORM: semukavic, sa alapen a pasa surur, saka taqed.
S.PHON: səmukaviʦ, sa aɭapən a pasa surur, saka taqəd.
S.TRANSL: He took off his load, took it to the bed platform and went to sleep.

S.FORM: "tekelu tua vava, kanu tua kinsa," nu aya a kina, "saqetju aken," aya sakamaya timadju.
S.PHON: "təkəɭu tua vava, kanu tua kinsa," nu aja a kina, "saqəcu akən," aja sakamaja timaɉu.
S.FORM: "tekelu tua vava, kanu tua kinsa," nu aya a kina, "saqetju aken," aya sakamaya timadju.
S.PHON: "təkəɭu tua vava, kanu tua kinsa," nu aja a kina, "saqəʦu akən," aja sakamaja timaɉu.
S.TRANSL: When his mother said: “Drink some beer, have some food,” he just said: “I’m ill”.

S.FORM: tapuluq katu lima inika keman tua kinsa.
S.PHON: tapuɭuq katu ɭima inika kəman tua kinsa.
S.FORM: tapuluq katu lima inika keman tua kinsa.
S.PHON: tapuɭuq katu ɭima inika kəman tua kinsa.
S.TRANSL: He didn’t eat for 15 days.

S.FORM: "Sapulungan, uzai a vavuy," aya qaliqali, "sa-u alapu."
S.PHON: "sapuɭuŋan, uzai a vavuj," aja qaɭiqaɭi, "sa-u aɭapu."
S.FORM: "Sapulungan, uzai a vavuy," aya qaliqali, "sa-u alapu."
S.PHON: "sapuɭuŋan, uzai a vavuj," aja qaɭiqaɭi, "sa-u aɭapu."
S.TRANSL: Some people said: “Sapulungan, there are some wild pigs; go and get them.”
Sapulungan went hunting.

S.FORM: parimasudj a kina tua qaqerengan nimadju; manu izua nuki anema.
S.PHON: parimasuɉ a kina tua qaqərəŋan nimaɉu; manu izua nuki anəma.
S.FORM: parimasudj a kina tua qaqerengan nimadju; manu izua nuki anema.
S.PHON: parimasuɉ a kina tua qaqərəŋan nimaɉu; manu izua nuki anəma.
S.TRANSL: His mother tidied his bed; and there was something there.

S.FORM: "manu mavan a saqetjuan nua ku aɫak," aya, sa veritsan a pasa tekutekuan.
S.PHON: "manu mavan a saqəcuan nua ku aʎak," aja, sa vəriʦan a pasa təkutəkuan.
S.FORM: "manu mavan a saqetjuan nua ku aljak," aya, sa verican a pasa tekutekuan.
S.PHON: "manu mavan a saqəʦuan nua ku aʎak," aja, sa vəriʦan a pasa təkutəkuan.
S.TRANSL: “So that was my son’s illness,” she said, and threw it down below (to the rubbish heap).

S.FORM: mangtjez ti Sapulungan; "ainu a ku..?" aya.
S.PHON: maŋcəz ti sapuɭuŋan; "ainu a ku..ʔ" aja.
S.FORM: mangtjez ti Sapulungan; "ainu a ku..?" aya.
S.PHON: maŋʦəz ti sapuɭuŋan; "ainu a ku..?" aja.
S.TRANSL: Sapulungan came back. “Where is my..?” he said.
“What do you mean?
Your flute?
Your mouth-organ?”
“That’s not what I mean.”

S.FORM: "mavan ka su maya a ku siniverits."
S.PHON: "mavan ka su maja a ku sinivəriʦ."
S.FORM: "mavan ka su maya a ku siniveric."
S.PHON: "mavan ka su maja a ku sinivəriʦ."
S.TRANSL: “Perhaps you mean what I threw away.”
“Where did you throw it, mother?”
“I threw it down below.”

S.FORM: qau vaik ti Sapulungan sema zua.
S.PHON: qau vaik ti sapuɭuŋan səma zua.
S.FORM: qau vaik ti Sapulungan sema zua.
S.PHON: qau vaik ti sapuɭuŋan səma zua.
S.TRANSL: So Sapulungan went down there.

S.FORM: manu izua ti Muakai, a naqemiladj i tekutekuan.
S.PHON: manu izua ti muakai, a naqəmiɭaɉ i təkutəkuan.
S.FORM: manu izua ti Muakai, a naqemiladj i tekutekuan.
S.PHON: manu izua ti muakai, a naqəmiɭaɉ i təkutəkuan.
S.TRANSL: And there was Muakai, sitting there down below (having turned into a human being (OA)).

S.FORM: alapen ni Sapulungan a temavikiɫ, a pasa kaɫuvuɫuvung.
S.PHON: aɭapən ni sapuɭuŋan a təmavikiʎ, a pasa kaʎuvuʎuvuŋ.
S.FORM: alapen ni Sapulungan a temavikilj, a pasa kaljuvuljuvung.
S.PHON: aɭapən ni sapuɭuŋan a təmavikiʎ, a pasa kaʎuvuʎuvuŋ.
S.TRANSL: Sapulungan took her and embraced her, and took her to the resting platform.
“I’ll give her a kiss,” said his mother.
Then there was an earthquake.

S.FORM: malap a kina tua qata, sa pavayan tjai Muakai, sana kisangutj, sana pavay tua anemanemanga siniputsekeɫ tjaimadju, sa pavayan tua matjaɫaɫak ni Muakai.
S.PHON: maɭap a kina tua qata, sa pavajan cai muakai, sana kisaŋuc, sana pavaj tua anəmanəmaŋa sinipuʦəkəʎ caimaɉu, sa pavajan tua macaʎaʎak ni muakai.
S.FORM: malap a kina tua qata, sa pavayan tjai Muakai, sana kisangutj, sana pavay tua anemanemanga sinipucekelj tjaimadju, sa pavayan tua matjaljaljak ni Muakai.
S.PHON: maɭap a kina tua qata, sa pavajan ʦai muakai, sana kisaŋuʦ, sana pavaj tua anəmanəmaŋa sinipuʦəkəʎ ʦaimaɉu, sa pavajan tua maʦaʎaʎak ni muakai.
S.TRANSL: His mother took precious beads and gave them to Muakai, and kissed her, and gave all sorts of wedding gifts for her, and gave them to Muakai’s parents.

S.FORM: "ai, manu izainu anga a tja aɫak?" aya tiamadju a maḍusa, sana vaik a sema zua ɫemiɫaw tjai Muakai.
S.PHON: "ai, manu izainu aŋa a ca aʎakʔ" aja tiamaɉu a maɖusa, sana vaik a səma zua ʎəmiʎaw cai muakai.
S.FORM: "ai, manu izainu anga a tja aljak?" aya tiamadju a madrusa, sana vaik a sema zua ljemiljaw tjai Muakai.
S.PHON: "ai, manu izainu aŋa a ʦa aʎak?" aja tiamaɉu a maɖusa, sana vaik a səma zua ʎəmiʎaw ʦai muakai.
S.TRANSL: “Oh, so where has our child got to?” they said, and went there to visit Muakai.

S.FORM: puaɫak anga ti Muakai kati Sapulungan, saka malevanga tiamadju.
S.PHON: puaʎak aŋa ti muakai kati sapuɭuŋan, saka maɭəvaŋa tiamaɉu.
S.FORM: pualjak anga ti Muakai kati Sapulungan, saka malevanga tiamadju.
S.PHON: puaʎak aŋa ti muakai kati sapuɭuŋan, saka maɭəvaŋa tiamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: Muakai and Sapulungan had a child and were very happy.
The end.
